tools.description: Read one POSE spec by slug: lifecycle frontmatter (status, created_at, completed_at, supersedes, depends_on, priority) plus the full markdown body.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_spec

tools.description: Bidirectional requirement-to-evidence trace of one POSE spec: every declared R-ID with its trace disposition (satisfied, waived, withdrawn), evidence text and structured refs (check:, test:, report:, commit:), plus the reverse evidence→requirements index, missing and orphaned entries.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

slug.description: Spec slug whose requirement trace to project

tools.name: pose_requirement_trace

tools.description: Append-only amendment history of one POSE spec: material requirement changes with affected R-IDs, rationale, author/reviewer aliases and timestamps, plus any current requirement state not yet acknowledged by an amendment event.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

slug.description: Spec slug whose amendment history to read

tools.name: pose_spec_amendments

tools.description: List every POSE spec of the project with its lifecycle frontmatter (no body). Optionally filter by status: draft, in-progress, done, blocked, superseded or abandoned.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous call's next_cursor; omit for the first page. Treat as opaque — do not parse or construct it.

limit.description: Optional page size; omit to return every item in one page (default, unpaginated — preserves the pre-pagination response shape)

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_list_specs

tools.description: Answer whether a POSE spec is eligible for work/execution: not in a terminal status and every depends_on ref satisfied (spec refs need status done; milestone:/roadmap: refs are fail-closed until roadmaps are projected). Returns {ready, status, waiting_on: [{ref, reason}], reason}. Consult before acting on a spec.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_spec_readiness

tools.description: Read the changelog state (pose-release-changelog): pending unreleased fragments (one per delivered spec) and consolidated release versions; pass a version to load its full body.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

version.description: Optional release version (e.g. "v0.2.0") to load its consolidated changelog

tools.name: pose_get_changelog

tools.description: List every governed roadmap (pose-roadmap-artifact): status, depends_on and milestones (id, after, target dates, specs). The order of N specs lives here.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous call's next_cursor; omit for the first page. Treat as opaque — do not parse or construct it.

limit.description: Optional page size; omit to return every item in one page (default, unpaginated — preserves the pre-pagination response shape)

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_list_roadmaps

tools.description: Read one governed roadmap by slug: frontmatter, milestones (with planned dates and member specs) and the markdown body.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_roadmap

tools.description: Canonical POSE trail for a task type (workflow + skill + cumulative rules + validation command), straight from the deterministic CLI (pose suggest --json). Use before starting any task to know which workflow and rules apply. Optional domain or repo-relative path refine the rule set.

domain.description: Optional domain for extra rules (e.g. frontend, backend-go, k8s)

path.description: Optional repo-relative path to infer the domain from

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

task_type.description: Task type, e.g. feature, bugfix, refactor, documentation, adr, knowledge, recurrence-escalation

tools.name: pose_suggest

tools.description: Read one POSE workflow (.pose/workflows/<name>.md) with its full checklist and execution modes. Without a name, lists every available workflow.

name.description: Workflow name, e.g. feature, bugfix, review; omit to list all

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_workflow

tools.description: Read one POSE domain rule (.pose/rules/<domain>.md). Without a domain, lists every available rule. Rules apply cumulatively; in conflict the most restrictive (usually security) prevails.

domain.description: Rule domain, e.g. security, backend-go, frontend-react; omit to list all

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_rules

tools.description: Aggregate local POSE report history into deterministic outcome insights. Returns the same structured contract as pose stats --json, without network access or writes.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

since_days.description: Optional rolling window in days; zero includes all history

tools.name: pose_insights

tools.description: Live backlog of spec follow-ups (pose followups --json): open items with their source spec, disposition and lexical near-duplicate candidates. Input for planning the next specs.

all.description: true = every follow-up (any disposition); default false = open backlog only

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_followups

tools.description: Evaluate the POSE structural integrity gate (pose check) in read-only mode. Returns the verdict (passed/exit_code) plus the full output as evidence — a failing gate is a result, not an error.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

strict.description: Strict mode (default true); tolerant turns failures into warnings

tools.name: pose_check

tools.description: Evaluate the spec content + lifecycle gate (pose lint-spec) in read-only mode: skeletal sections, done-without-completed_at, follow-ups without disposition. Without a slug, evaluates every spec.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

slug.description: Spec slug; omit to lint all specs

tools.name: pose_lint_spec

tools.description: List POSE knowledge entries (handoffs, decision-logs, notes) from .pose/knowledge/. Excludes sensitivity:restricted entries. Returns metadata only; use pose_get_knowledge for the full body.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous call's next_cursor; omit for the first page. Treat as opaque — do not parse or construct it.

limit.description: Optional page size; omit to return every item in one page (default, unpaginated — preserves the pre-pagination response shape)

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_list_knowledge

tools.description: Get one POSE knowledge entry by slug (full body). Returns error if the entry has sensitivity:restricted.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_knowledge

tools.description: List all historical POSE compliance and validation reports from .pose/reports/history/*.jsonl. Returns structured execution metadata, excluding full report bodies.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous call's next_cursor; omit for the first page. Treat as opaque — do not parse or construct it.

limit.description: Optional page size; omit to return every item in one page (default, unpaginated — preserves the pre-pagination response shape)

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_list_reports

tools.description: Get the full markdown content of a POSE validation report by its filename from .pose/reports/.

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_report

tools.description: Read one agent skill by name (.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md): full SKILL.md body with trigger keywords, steps, and output requirements. Without a name, lists every available skill. Use before executing a skill-driven task (e.g. pose-investigate) to get the exact execution protocol.

name.description: Skill name, e.g. "pose-investigate"; omit to list all

project_id.description: Optional project to scope the .pose root (multi-project); omit for the default root

tools.name: pose_get_skill

tools.description: Open an observed external run on the Conductor board. Call this at the start of a session to create a task visible in the board. Returns run_id and task_id to pass to subsequent conductor_run_event / conductor_run_close calls. Requires CONDUCTOR_URL, CONDUCTOR_PROJECT_ID (and optionally CONDUCTOR_RUN_TOKEN) to be set when pose-mcp was started.

adapter.description: Reporter adapter identifier, e.g. 'claude-code', 'codex'. Defaults to 'mcp'

spec_slug.description: POSE spec slug this run is associated with, e.g. 'portal-session-principal'

title.description: Human-readable title for the run (e.g. 'Fix auth bug in session-store')

tools.name: conductor_run_open

tools.description: Append an event to an open external run on the Conductor board. Use to report intermediate progress (tool calls, cost increments, checkpoints). Terminal event types (run.succeeded, run.failed, run.canceled) also close the run — prefer conductor_run_close for that.

cost_usd.description: Incremental cost in USD for this event (optional)

payload.description: Arbitrary JSON payload for the event (will be redacted server-side)

tools.name: conductor_run_event

tools.description: Close an observed external run on the Conductor board by emitting a terminal event. The run's task moves to CLOSED / FAILED / CANCELED. Call this at the end of a session.

cost_usd.description: Total cost in USD for the run (optional)

tools.name: conductor_run_close
